summary: Implemented Calendly → Zoffice Consultant integration as a real webhook receiver + stateful job scheduler. Adds signature verification (when CALENDLY_WEBHOOK_SIGNING_KEY is set), session/job persistence under N5/config/consulting/calendly_zoffice, notification queue emission, and a new `deactivate <worker>` command in zoffice_worker to support post-session shutdown.
tests:
python3 - <<'PY' | python3 Integrations/calendly-zoffice/webhook_handler.py
import json
from datetime import datetime, timedelta, timezone
start=(datetime.now(timezone.utc)+timedelta(minutes=30)).isoformat()
payload={"event_type":"Zoffice Consulting","start_time":start,"invitee":{"email":"client@example.com","name":"Client Example"}}
print(json.dumps(payload))
PY
notes: Webhook server accepts POST /api/calendly/webhook (also /webhook and /). Signature verification uses Calendly-Webhook-Signature header (t=...,v1=...) and CALENDLY_WEBHOOK_SIGNING_KEY.
This drop queues outbound notifications (prep email + SMS) to notifications.jsonl; delivery requires a separate notifier agent/service on zoputer.
To fully automate, schedule: `python3 Integrations/calendly-zoffice/scripts/session_prep.py tick` every 1 minute on zoputer.
Manual override supported via CLI: `python3 Integrations/calendly-zoffice/scripts/session_prep.py hold|resume` (SMS command wiring still needed).
